code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    All predicates are rendered with appropriate verb phrases.  When all triples share
    the same subject they are combined into one complex sentence; otherwise each triple
    becomes a short clause.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper: strip surrounding quotes from objects
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip('\"')

    # Mapping from predicate to a phrase generator (subject, object) -> clause
    phrase_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": lambda s, o: f"features {clean_obj(o)} architecture",
        "buildingStartDate": lambda s, o: f"started construction in {clean_obj(o)}",
        "completionDate": lambda s, o: f"was completed in {clean_obj(o)}",
        "floorCount": lambda s, o: f"has {clean_obj(o)} floors",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"is located at {clean_obj(o)}",
        "cost": lambda s, o: f"costs {clean_obj(o)}",
        "floorArea": lambda s, o: f"has a floor area of {clean_obj(o)} square metres",
        "architect": lambda s, o: f"was designed by {clean_obj(o)}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"is owned by {clean_obj(o)}",
        "formerName": lambda s, o: f"was formerly known as {clean_obj(o)}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"is {clean_obj(o)} metres tall",
        "buildingType": lambda s, o: f"is a {clean_obj(o)}",
        "developer": lambda s, o: f"was developed by {clean_obj(o)}",
        "tenant": lambda s, o: f"has tenant {clean_obj(o)}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"is part of {clean_obj(o)}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"is in {clean_obj(o)}",
        "currentTenants": lambda s, o: f"currently houses {clean_obj(o)}",
        "address": lambda s, o: f"is located at {clean_obj(o)}",
        "inaugurationDate": lambda s, o: f"was inaugurated on {clean_obj(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"was born in {clean_obj(o)}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"died in {clean_obj(o)}",
        "bedCount": lambda s, o: f"has {clean_obj(o)} beds",
        "region": lambda s, o: f"is in the region of {clean_obj(o)}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"is in the state of {clean_obj(o)}",
        "website": lambda s, o: f"has website {clean_obj(o)}",
        "yearOfConstruction": lambda s, o: f"was built in {clean_obj(o)}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": lambda s, o: f"has NRHP reference number {clean_obj(o)}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": lambda s, o: f"was added to the NRHP on {clean_obj(o)}",
        "significantBuilding": lambda s, o: f"is known for {clean_obj(o)}",
        "governingBody": lambda s, o: f"is governed by {clean_obj(o)}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"is led by {clean_obj(o)}",
        "chancellor": lambda s, o: f"has chancellor {clean_obj(o)}",
        "governmentType": lambda s, o: f"has government type {clean_obj(o)}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"has capital {clean_obj(o)}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"speaks {clean_obj(o)}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"has leader title {clean_obj(o)}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"uses the {clean_obj(o)}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"has ethnic group {clean_obj(o)}",
        "origin": lambda s, o: f"originates from {clean_obj(o)}",
        "significantProject": lambda s, o: f"has significant project {clean_obj(o)}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"was founded in {clean_obj(o)}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"people are called {clean_obj(o)}",
        "numberOfRooms": lambda s, o: f"has {clean_obj(o)} rooms",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"has key person {clean_obj(o)}",
        "architecture": lambda s, o: f"features architecture {clean_obj(o)}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"has postal code {clean_obj(o)}",
    }

    # Determine whether all triples share the same subject
    subjects = {t.subject for t in triples}
    if len(subjects) == 1:
        subject = triples[0].subject
        clauses = []
        for t in triples:
            generator = phrase_map.get(t.predicate, None)
            if generator:
                clauses.append(generator(subject, t.object))
            else:
                # fallback to a generic phrasing
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                clauses.append(f"{pred} {clean_obj(t.object)}")
        # Combine clauses with commas and an 'and' before the last one
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            sentence = f"{subject} {clauses[0]}."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject} " + ', '.join(clauses[:-1]) + f", and {clauses[-1]}."
        return sentence
    else:
        # Different subjects – produce a short clause for each triple
        parts = []
        for t in triples:
            generator = phrase_map.get(t.predicate, None)
            if generator:
                parts.append(f"{t.subject} {generator(t.subject, t.object)}")
            else:
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                parts.append(f"{t.subject} {pred} {clean_obj(t.object)}")
        return '; '.join(parts) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single, fluent sentence.
      If all triples share the same subject, the sentence lists the attributes
      of that subject; otherwise each triple is expressed separately.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Determine whether all triples refer to the same subject
      subjects = {t.subject for t in triples}
      if len(subjects) == 1:
          subject = triples[0].subject
          parts = []
          for t in triples:
              # make predicate more readable
              pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
              parts.append(f"{pred} {t.object}")
          # Join attributes with commas and an 'and' before the last item
          if len(parts) > 1:
              attrs = ', '.join(parts[:-1]) + f", and {parts[-1]}"
          else:
              attrs = parts[0]
          return f"{subject} has {attrs}."
      else:
          # Different subjects – produce a short clause for each triple
          clauses = []
          for t in triples:
              pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
              clauses.append(f"{t.subject} {pred} {t.object}")
